posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The U.S. is actively supporting shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz via an alternative Omani route to maintain critical energy supply flows, as evidenced by increased naval presence monitoring vessels transiting the passage. This effort suggests Washington is working to mitigate disruption risks to global oil markets amid ongoing regional tensions.

posts.text:
Norway oil service lockout takes effect, disrupts offshore drilling

Around 1,000 Norwegian oil service workers were locked out on Saturday morning in an escalation of a labour dispute that ​is expected to disrupt drilling and some production on the Norwegian continental ‌shelf.
posts.summary: A lockout of approximately 1,000 Norwegian oil service workers has begun, expected to disrupt offshore drilling and production activities on the Norwegian continental shelf. This labor dispute carries potential implications for oil supply disruptions in the region.

posts.text:
Rystad Energy sees Middle East oil flows fully recovering before the end of the year.

Why? 

1. Most large oil production and export assets were left unscathed. 

2. After months of lost income, Middle East oil producers are now more incentivized than ever to ramp oil production
posts.summary: Middle East oil production is expected to return to normal levels by year-end, supported by minimal damage to major facilities and strong financial incentives for producers to increase output following recent revenue losses.
